title: The 15-minute city quantified using human mobility data

data_scope.primary_panel: ~40 million US smartphone users (~10% of US smartphones)
data_scope.pois_total: 5.3 million POIs (3.9 million in study urban areas)
data_scope.poi_categories: 9 essential categories (restaurants, schools, parks, healthcare, drugstores, arts/culture, grocery stores, services, religious organizations)

core_conclusion: The median US resident makes only 14% of daily consumption trips within a 15-minute walk from home. Local amenity access explains 84% of variation in local-trip behaviour across 420 urban areas and 74% within cities. A 1961 NYC zoning natural experiment supports a causal link from access to local usage. However, promoting local living increases experienced income segregation for low-income residents, who encounter far less socioeconomic diversity on short trips than on longer ones.
why_it_matters: First large-scale US study linking amenity access to observed GPS trip behaviour; credible causal IV design; reveals equity trade-off that reshapes 15-minute city policy design; national scale (420 urban areas, 13.6B POI visits) reveals strong Northeast/South regional divide; directly informs land-use and zoning reform as a climate tool.
why_nature: GPS-revealed actual behaviour from 40M devices elevates field from potential to revealed accessibility use; credible quasi-experimental causal design rare in urban mobility; addresses climate policy, urban equity, and land-use regulation; surprising counter-narrative challenges uncritical 15-minute city enthusiasm.
added_value_to_my_research: GPS-revealed trip behaviour as supervision for calibrating 2SFCA/R2SFCA catchment radii and distance-decay functions from observed data
Mobility bipartite network + community detection to diagnose structurally segregated service sub-markets with explainable ML attribution
Multi-city zoning quasi-experiment panel via AI-assisted archive mining — GeoAI pipeline for causal policy evaluation
Experienced-segregation index recast as bi-level optimization objective: jointly maximize 15-min usage and minimize income isolation in facility placement
Transfer-learning from US SafeGraph labels to satellite proxies for globally applicable 15-minute city diagnostics in data-scarce cities
